conjecture: A physically accurate image of a neutron star can be rendered from first principles through an unbroken chain of equations: TOV structure (M,R,g) → dipole surface model T(θ,B) → self-consistent magnetised radiative transfer (Feautrier, two polarisation modes) → Schwarzschild geodesic ray tracing → spectral integration → CIE colorimetry → tone-mapped sRGB, where every equation is traceable to a locally-stored peer-reviewed source.
author: Claude+Tobias
